notes: [liveness] fx-deathtypes.message.special_nade_misc: DEATH_SHOOTING_STAR has a selector (SelectSpecial maps DeathTypes.ShootingStar -> SHOOTING_STAR) but NO live emitter in the port: no jumppad/launch volume tags a kill with it, so the path is dead. Latent-only (Base registers it but it is a niche death). All other rows in this family are now live.

[liveness] fx-deathtypes.message.teamchange: DEATH_TEAMCHANGE (manual team change) has no live emitter in the port: every force-kill team change routes through DeathTypes.AutoTeamChange (Teamplay.cs:698). The AUTOTEAMCHANGE branch IS live; the manual TEAMCHANGE self-line is selectable but dead. Minor — the rendered text differs only by the auto/manual wording.

[liveness] fx-deathtypes.message.mapper_custom: DEATH_CUSTOM emitter is DEAD: the selector + emit path are now fully wired (DeathTypes.Custom const, Scores.EmitObituary CUSTOM branch with the 'You were %s' formatting matching damage.qc:457), but nothing in the port tags a death with DeathTypes.Custom (grep finds only the consumer at Scores.cs:610) — no mapper trigger_hurt/deathmessage emitter, so the path never fires.

[logic] fx-notifications.net.shouldsend: STILL OPEN (unchanged this wave): NotificationReaches (:2399-2417) omits the QC spectator-follow clause: NOTIF_ONE should also reach `IS_SPEC(to) && to.enemy == other`; NOTIF_TEAM the spec whose `enemy.team == other.team`; NOTIF_ALL_EXCEPT must also exclude `IS_SPEC(to) && to.enemy == other`; NOTIF_TEAM_EXCEPT has a parallel spec nuance. The port covers only the direct player match for each, so a spectator following a player will not receive that player's personal NOTIF_ONE/TEAM notifications. ;

[liveness] fx-sounds.emit.sound_allowed: SoundAllowedGate.WalkOwner (owner-walk re-home) is implemented but DEAD: no caller uses it to re-home corpse/projectile sounds to the originating player before the gate check. ; IsAllowed (bot_sound_monopoly gate) is live only on the vehicle hit/shield emit paths (VehicleCommon.cs:502/511/522); it is NOT applied on the general one-shot emit path (SoundService.Play / PlayOn), so most SVQC emits bypass the gate. ; QC MSG_ONE 'sounds to self always pass' branch omitted (no per-recipient msg_entity

[liveness] fx-sounds.emit.play2_family: play2team (Play2Team) is ported but DEAD — zero live callers. Base calls play2team for ONS_CONTROLPOINT_UNDERATTACK / ONS_GENERATOR_UNDERATTACK (sv_onslaught.qc:386/943); the port emits those via PlayGlobal instead (OnslaughtControlPoint.cs:249), a per-team -> global broadcast divergence. ; play2all port (PlayGlobal) omits QC play2all's `if(autocvar_bot_sound_monopoly) return;` gate, so global cues still play when bot_sound_monopoly=1. ; Many Base play2 call sites remain unwired in the port: wea

[logic] fx-sounds.voice.globalsound_dispatch: AUTOTAUNT per-recipient probability roll (tauntrand < CS_CVAR(it).cl_autotaunt) still omitted: per-client cl_autotaunt is not networked, so autotaunts that clear sv_autotaunt always play for all recipients (documented deferral; a single global cl_autotaunt default 0 would wrongly silence ALL autotaunts). ; TAUNT does not trigger animdecide_setaction(ANIMACTION_TAUNT) on the living speaker (no anim-action seam yet).

[values] fx-sounds.presentation.hitsound_sample: Pitch curve simplified: the port uses a linear damage->pitch ramp (mode 2/3) instead of QC's asymptotic gradient function (a=cl_hitsound_max_pitch, b=cl_hitsound_min_pitch, c=cl_hitsound_nom_damage); audible only as a slightly different pitch shape, and only for cl_hitsound 2/3 (default 1 = fixed pitch, identical).

[logic] items-pickups.loot.item_damage: StartItem sets loot TakeDamage=DamageMode.Yes (SetupLoot l.202, with a 'event_damage = Item_Damage' comment) but NO Item_Damage event handler is wired — so a loot item pushed into a lava/sky-kill volume by damage is never removed. The only NODROP kill is the at-spawn origin trace (SetupLoot l.214-223); loot that drifts into NODROP after spawn, or is struck by a NEEDKILL deathtype, survives.

[logic] items-pickups.target.use_setactive: A targetname'd item starts hidden (Item_Reset handles that — ported), but there is no use/trigger path to make it appear: QC sets item.use = item_use so a trigger firing the item's targetname spawns it (spawnflags&16 -> touch immediately, else respawn). The port wires no .use handler on items, so trigger_-activated map items never appear. item_setactive (the active/toggle relay used by relays/triggers) is likewise unported.

[logic] items-pickups.team.finditem: Maps that group several items under one .team key (so only ONE of the group is present at a time, randomly chosen, re-rolled on each respawn) are not supported: StartItem calls Item_Reset for every item unconditionally (the `if(team) Item_FindTeam else Item_Reset` branch collapses to the else). All grouped items spawn and respawn independently.

[logic/liveness] mutator-bloodloss.presentation.active_mutator_signalling: The util.qc:309 X("Blood loss", MUT_BLOODLOSS, g_bloodloss>0) GameType active-mutator row and the CSQC mut_set_active(MUT_BLOODLOSS) HUD active-mutator icon are STILL entirely unported — the port has no MUT_BLOODLOSS / mut_set_active / active_mutators flag surface at all, so the menu/server-browser HUD active-mutator ICON can never light up (the textual 'modifications' string IS now surfaced, but not the bitmask/icon). ; BuildMutatorsString (':bloodloss') is LIVE but only via the gamelog/eventlo

The ForbidThrowCurrentWeapon hook has live Call sites (WeaponThrowing.cs:149/185). ; Divergences from QC: uses center-distance + Api.Entities.FindInRadius instead of WarpZone_FindRadius (no warpzone traversal); omits Drop_Special_Items(closest) (flag/key carriers keep their objective on

EF_NOSHADOW IS set on the player while held (ApplyBuff:461), but the glow model itself is absent.

[logic/values/timing] buffs.buff_effect: No per-carrier Send_Effect particle emission throttled by buff_effect_delay; g_buffs_effects (default ON) is never read. EntityMutatorState declares a buff_effect_delay field but nothing emits the effect.

[logic/values/timing] buffs.waypoint: No WP_Buff radar/HUD waypoint on buff items (no buff_Waypoint_Spawn, no RADARICON_Buff, no build-finished timer); the colored buff icon never appears on radar/screen edge, and there is no per-viewer hide-if-already-held logic.

[logic/values] buffs.replace_powerups: Still no FilterItem hook in BuffsMutator: with g_buffs>=0 and g_buffs_replace_powerups, QC replaces every powerup item with a random buff (buff_SpawnReplacement). The g_buffs>0 'replace' mode is entirely non-functional, removing the second obtain path. (A FilterItemDefinition hook chain exists in MutatorHooks.cs but BuffsMutator does not subscribe it.)

[logic] buffs.observer_cleanup: Still no MakePlayerObserver / ClientDisconnect handler to run buffs_RemovePlayer (delete the carrier buff_model). Moot only because the buff_model glow itself is unimplemented (buffs.carrier_model).

The port treats bugrigs as server-authoritative so this is LIKELY inert, but that is unverified under the port's net smoothing / cl_movement_errorcompensation layer — could surface as camera/position drift for a bugrigs client.

[liveness] random_gravity.roll.round_gate: Liveness is partial-by-design: RoundNotStartedProvider is wired only inside GameWorld.EnableRounds (GameWorld.cs:1134), i.e. only for round-based gametypes (CA/Freezetag/round Domination/…). In non-round modes the provider is null and RoundGateBlocks() returns false — which exactly matches QC where round_handler_IsActive() is false outside round modes. So the gate is correctly inert where Base is inert, and active where Base is active. Not a defect, just mode-scoped reachability.

(Unchanged from baseline — still open, minor.)

[liveness] smneg.mutator_string: BuildMutatorsPrettyString chain is DEAD: MutatorActivation.BuildMutatorsPrettyString exists and the smneg override returns ', Stale-move negation', but no server code invokes the pretty-string chain (QC server/client.qc:1107 votescreen/scoreboard consumer not ported). So the pretty label never reaches any UI.

[values] status_effects.framework.registry: StatusEffectDef still drops m_color/m_icon/m_name (HUD reconstructs label/icon/color from a separate PowerupMeta table in PowerupsPanel). The behavioral methods (RemovalSound/PersistentCheck/OnTick/OnRemove) are all carried, and burning now also carries OnRemove (BurningRemove clears EF_FLAME) — only the cosmetic m_color/m_icon/m_name remain dropped.

[logic] touchexplode.list.activemutators: Base appends 'Touch explode' to the active_mutators() X-table string (util.qc:315, MUT_TOUCHEXPLODE bitfield) used for the server-browser mutator field and the scoreboard/info mutator list. The port has no active_mutators()/MUT_-bitfield string builder. NOTE: the port's newer BuildMutatorsString/BuildMutatorsPrettyString hook infra (MutatorActivation.cs:108/121) is a DIFFERENT mechanism (the per-mutator server-info/scoreboard token chain), and Base touchexplode registers NO BuildMutatorsString h

[liveness] waypoints.registry.defs: Residual: the wp_extra-DRIVEN spritelookupblinkvalue overrides (superweapon Weapon=2, Item=item.m_waypointblink) are not modeled — Weapon's def blink is a flat 1 and there is no per-instance wp_extra blink lookup. Harmless while those producers (superweapon pickups) are absent. The Seeker/Item/Weapon DEFS are now exercised by live producers; only the wp_extra per-instance blink override is unmodeled. ; liveness: partial — defs are reachable only once their gametype emits waypoints; CTF + Dominat

[liveness] waypoints.server.manager_api: DEPLOY + CLEAR + HELPME slice is live (DeployFixed/DeployPersonal/Attach/ClearPersonal/ClearOwned/HelpMePing from Commands.cs; Reset() + Think() in GameWorld; SendWaypoints merges WaypointSprites.Active per peer). ; OBJECTIVE/UPDATE slice is BROADLY live now: SpawnFixed/Spawn/UpdateMaxHealth/UpdateHealth/Disown/Kill via Assault (SpawnObjectiveSprite); Spawn/UpdateBuildFinished/Ping/Kill via itemstime (ItemPickupRules.RespawnCountdown, ItemPickupRules.cs:757/768/783); Spawn/UpdateRule/Kill via Se

[logic/liveness] waypoints.gametype.collect: Seven live CollectWaypoints producers now: CTF (flag base/dropped/carrier), Domination (control-point markers), FreezeTag (Frozen/Reviving + thaw-progress health bar), Keepaway (KaBall/KaBallCarrier, tracking-gated), TeamKeepaway (team-colored TkaBallCarrier<team>/KaBall, tracking-gated), Key Hunt (KeyDropped + KeyCarrier<team>/KeyCarrierFinish, tracking-gated per-viewer predicates), LMS (LmsLeader on each leader, show-window gated). Plus Assault via the manager API, and itemstime/Seeker/powerup

[logic] waypoints.visibility.three_image_rule: Port still networks a single SpriteName; the own-team/enemy-team/spectator image triple (model1/2/3) and the client-side SPRITERULE_TEAMPLAY image selection are not modeled.

[logic] waypoints.weapon.whereis: Weapon_whereis is not ported: when a player presses a weapon key for a weapon they don't own (and g_showweaponspawns != 0), Base spawns a short-lived WP_Weapon sprite over every matching weapon-spawn item (wp_extra = the weapon id for client icon resolution), RADARICON_NONE so in-world only. The port has no equivalent — no g_showweaponspawns cvar wiring and no whereis producer. WP_Weapon is otherwise live only via the itemstime respawn countdown.

[logic] resources.api.setresource: Base's SetResource hook reads back BOTH res_type (M_ARGV(8) entity) and amount (M_ARGV(9)) after the forbid check, so a mutator may rewrite the RESOURCE TYPE as well as the amount. The port's CallSetResource (ResourceHooks.cs:57-64) only rewrites the amount (ref float); res_type is fixed. No shipped mutator rewrites res_type, so latent.

[logic] resources.api.take: TakeResourceWithLimit STILL does NOT fire its own QC mutator hook (sv_resources.qc:211 MUTATOR_CALLHOOK(TakeResourceWithLimit)) — Resources.cs:172-182 clamps the drain and delegates to TakeResource but never calls a ResourceHooks chain (comment only; ResourceHooks has no TakeResourceWithLimit chain). It does delegate to TakeResource (which now DOES fire the TakeResource hook), so the inner hook still runs; only the WithLimit-specific hook is absent. No shipped mutator subscribes, so latent.
